chunk_summary: Adolescent vitamin D and calcium deficiency management focuses on diagnosis, treatment, and prevention through dietary intake, supplementation, and sunlight exposure, guided by IAP protocols and CNNS findings.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of diagnosing vitamin D and calcium deficiency in adolescents, including syndromic evaluations.
Learn treatment protocols for vitamin D deficiency and nutritional rickets, including specific dosages and regimens.
Recognize the role of dietary calcium sources and safe sunlight exposure in prevention and maintenance.
key_facts: Vitamin D deficiency affects a substantial minority of Indian adolescents, as per CNNS 2016–18 findings.
Treatment for vitamin D deficiency includes daily cholecalciferol (3000–6000 IU/day) or intermittent regimens (60,000 IU weekly for 6 weeks) with calcium supplementation (500–800 mg/day).
Maintenance dosing is essential after deficiency treatment, with dietary calcium and sunlight exposure emphasized.
Repeated unsupervised high-dose vitamin D sachets can lead to toxicity; reassessment is critical.
Food-first calcium sources include milk, curd, paneer, ragi, millets, and fortified foods.
reference_quote: IAP guidance provides adolescent-appropriate options for nutritional rickets and vitamin D deficiency, including daily cholecalciferol (about 3000–6000 IU/day for 1–18 years) or an intermittent regimen (60,000 IU weekly for 6 weeks) with concurrent calcium supplementation.
document_key_topics: Vitamin D deficiency diagnosis and treatment
